behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Core correctness depends on real vision-model judgment (explicit vs non-explicit). A stubbed classifier would hide failures in model decisions, so unit/integration-only signals are insufficient. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior specifies a single model-dependent moderation decision, not a required multi-step agentic sequence across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated explicit-image set through the real moderation path using the real vision model, then assert each submission is flagged and lands in held-for-review state (and not in published state).

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This tests robustness/quality of real text-model classification under adversarial obfuscation; deterministic mocks will pass while missing true model weaknesses. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because behavior text targets one model classification outcome, not chained model decisions across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run an obfuscated hate-speech corpus (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing tricks) through the real text moderation model and verify posts are flagged/held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is externally visible side effect correctness (publish vs hold/drop), which requires round-trip observation at an external boundary. Unit/integration can miss silent-drop behavior despite internal flags. I considered Agentic, but rejected it because behavior text gives the precondition "passes both content checks" and does not require validating model quality, only end-to-end publish side effect under that precondition.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a known-safe post via the real submission interface, then verify through real readback (e.g., public feed/status API) that it is published and absent from held queue.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple sequential model-dependent stages (image model, text model, final decision) and end-to-end latency across the chain. Single-step agentic or infra-only tests miss cross-step timing interactions and queueing effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute timed end-to-end submissions through the real pipeline with both media types present, measure wall-clock from submission to final decision visibility, and assert p95 (or required percentile) is <= 5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is an external error-path behavior for a real dependency outage/unavailability. Mocked failures can miss real transport/timeout/error-contract handling that causes accidental auto-approval. I considered Agentic, but rejected it because behavior text is about outage handling (infrastructure/error path), not correctness of model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability (e.g., endpoint blocked or forced timeout), submit image posts, and verify they are persisted in retry queue and not published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on consistency across repeated model-involved submissions over time (cross-step state anchor). This is more than one isolated model call: later outcomes must remain aligned with earlier outcomes within a window, so sequential agentic behavior must be validated. I considered Agentic, but rejected it because the behavior explicitly requires consistency across multiple submissions (multi-step sequence), not a one-off model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the configured short window through the real pipeline; assert decisions are consistent across attempts and any cache/window mechanism preserves consistent hold/publish outcome.
